checks.a_analysis: The output content accurately reflects the sections and text present in the source PDF. No fabricated paragraphs or sentences were identified. All sections such as MISSION, VISION AND PRIORITIES, QUICK FACTS, and 2022 ACCOMPLISHMENTS are grounded in the source text.

The source document contains no phone numbers or physical addresses. The only URL provided is 'NC.gov' in the accomplishments section, which is correctly represented in the output. No fabricated contact information was found.

All primary headings and their respective bulleted lists/paragraphs from both pages of the source PDF are present in the output. This includes MISSION, VISION AND PRIORITIES, BUDGET, WHAT WE DO, FUNDING, BOARDS AND COMMISSIONS, QUICK FACTS, and 2022 ACCOMPLISHMENTS.
The source document contains no tables. The information is structured as headings and bulleted lists, which are correctly handled in the output.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: Each section preserves the content found in the source. For example, the 'QUICK FACTS' list contains all 10 items, and the '2022 ACCOMPLISHMENTS' section contains all 14 paragraphs.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: Two minor transcription errors were found in the 'QUICK FACTS' section. First, a number was incorrectly transcribed as '6,00' instead of '600'. Second, the acronym 'CJLEADS' (Criminal Justice Law Enforcement Automated Data Services) was misspelled as 'CLJEADS'.
checks.b_evidence_source: 9,000+ healthcare facilities linked to NC HealthConnex; another 600 in onboarding... Criminal Justice Law Enforcement Automated Data Services (CJLEADS)
checks.b_evidence_output: 9,000+ healthcare facilities linked to NC HealthConnex; another 6,00 in onboarding... Criminal Justice Law Enforcement Automated Data Services (CLJEADS)

checks.a_analysis: The content in the JSON output matches the source document without any repeated or duplicated paragraphs, sections, or list items. Each block of text from the source appears exactly once in the structured output.

The document title is correctly identified as the root section title (H1 level). All other major section headers (MISSION, VISION AND PRIORITIES, etc.) are structured as subsections of this root, effectively functioning as H2 levels. There are no skips in heading levels (e.g., H1 to H3).
There are no tables present in the source document or the converted output.

The reading order in the output significantly diverges from a logical flow (either column-wise or row-wise). On page 1, the output jumps from the top-left section (MISSION) to the bottom-right section (VISION AND PRIORITIES), then moves upward through the right column (BUDGET, WHAT WE DO), before returning to the middle of the left column (FUNDING) and ending back in the middle-right (BOARDS AND COMMISSIONS) and finally bottom-left (QUICK FACTS). This sequence is disjointed and does not follow the source's intended organization.
checks.b_evidence_source: The logical reading order of Page 1 is MISSION (top-left), WHAT WE DO (top-right), FUNDING (mid-left), BOARDS AND COMMISSIONS (mid-right), BUDGET (mid-low right), QUICK FACTS (bottom-left), VISION AND PRIORITIES (bottom-right).
checks.b_evidence_output: The JSON sequence is MISSION (reading_order 2) -> VISION AND PRIORITIES (reading_order 3) -> BUDGET (reading_order 4) -> WHAT WE DO (reading_order 5) -> FUNDING (reading_order 6) -> BOARDS AND COMMISSIONS (reading_order 7) -> QUICK FACTS (reading_order 8).

checks.a_analysis: The lists found in the sections 'WHAT WE DO', 'BOARDS AND COMMISSIONS', 'VISION AND PRIORITIES', and 'QUICK FACTS' have been correctly identified and structured as unordered lists. The item counts and content for each list match the source PDF.
The only identifiable link text 'NC.gov' on page 2 has been correctly converted into a hyperlink in the JSON with the appropriate 'href' attribute. No fabricated or broken links were detected.

checks.a_analysis: The source PDF uses a two-column layout for the first page, while the HTML rendering uses a single-column layout. The reading order of the blocks has been rearranged in the HTML: sections that appeared at the bottom right of the PDF (VISION AND PRIORITIES, BUDGET) have been moved to follow the MISSION section at the top. This rearrangement appears to follow a logical grouping of introductory information (Mission, Vision, Budget) which is reflected in the provided OCR sequence. The sections are clearly labeled with headings, and the content remains easy to locate and understand in its new vertical flow. This is a standard and acceptable adaptation for responsive, accessible HTML.
The source document contains lists and bulleted sections, but no formal grid-based tables. Therefore, no table rendering issues exist.

There are no meaningful images, logos, or charts in the source PDF. The document relies on text and decorative colored background blocks. The decorative pixel-style graphic at the bottom of PDF page 2 is correctly omitted from the HTML as it is purely decorative and its absence improves accessibility.

Each content block from the source PDF (Mission, Vision, Budget, What We Do, Funding, Boards and Commissions, Quick Facts, and Accomplishments) appears exactly once in the HTML output. No duplications were observed.
